channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How to watch the NY-18 Debate: Ryan vs. Esposito
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Candidates in the race for New York’s 18th Congressional District will face off in a debate Wednesday night. Democratic incumbent Congressman Pat Ryan will take on Republican challenger Alison Esposito in a 60-minute debate moderated by PIX11’s Henry Rosoff.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, PIX 11

item.title: Former Sen. Tim Johnson, the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota, dies at 77
item.description: SIOUX FALLS, S.D. (AP) — Former Sen. Tim Johnson, a centrist who was the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota and who was adept at securing federal funding for projects back home during his nearly three decades in Washington, has died. He was 77.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has 9-point lead on most important issue to voters: poll
item.description: A majority of voters say the economy is the most important issue going into the presidential election, and former President Trump holds a 9-point lead over Vice President Kamala Harris on the topic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security's scheduled cost of living increase 'won't make a dent' for some retirees
item.description: Social Security recipients are expected to receive a smaller cost-of-living increase in January than in recent years as inflation moderates.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida Republican suggests Greene have ‘head examined’ over hurricane claims
item.description: Florida Republican Rep. Carlos A. Gimenez suggested Wednesday that Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) should have her “head examined” after she claimed humans can control the weather.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: As Gaza war drags past 1 year mark, hope fades for a deal to bring hostages home soon
item.description: For a year now, freeing the hostages taken by Hamas has been a top goal for Israel, but some 101 remain unaccounted for and the hope of a deal to get them home is waning quickly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Military board substantiates misconduct but declines to fire Marine who adopted Afghan orphan
item.description: CAMP LEJEUNE, N.C. (AP) — A U.S. Marine whose adoption of an Afghan war orphan has spurred a yearslong legal battle and raised alarms at the highest levels of government will remain on active duty. A three-member panel of Marines said Tuesday that while Maj.
item.dc:creator: JULIET LINDERMAN, CLAIRE GALOFARO and MARTHA MENDOZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden and Netanyahu hold their first conversation in weeks. Trump recently called the Israeli leader
item.description: President Joe Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu have held their first call in weeks.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump sets sights on Pennsylvania and Harris plans to head west as hurricane scrambles campaigning
item.description: Donald Trump is holding rallies in pivotal Pennsylvania while Vice President Kamala Harris prepares for another visit to the West.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Woodward book reveals Trump's calls with Putin and Biden's private remarks on Obama and Netanyahu
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump has had as many as seven private phone calls with Vladimir Putin since leaving office and secretly sent the Russian president COVID-19 test machines during the height of the pandemic, Bob Woodward reported in his new book, “War.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In battleground states, it's not just about winning — but also losing by less
item.description: In a state where every vote matters, both Democratic and Republican campaigns are not only trying to win in counties where they’re strongest, they’re also trying to lose by less.
item.dc:creator: Ari Shapiro, Mia Venkat, Karen Zamora, and Ashley Brown, MPR News

item.title: Trump-backed House candidate rips Biden's 'joke' border policy for inviting gang violence to swing state
item.description: Fox News Digital spoke to AZ GOP congressional candidate Abe Hamadeh and GOP Chairwoman Gina Swoboda who warned that transnational gangs are coming across the border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Democrats in close races try to show they hear voter concerns about immigration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In some of the closest House races in the country, Democratic candidates are leaning into an issue that Republicans have made a centerpiece of their efforts to expand their majority — immigration. Rep.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Early in-person voting begins in Arizona, drawing visits from the presidential campaigns
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Early in-person voting begins Wednesday in Arizona, making it the first of this year's presidential battleground states where all residents can cast a ballot at a traditional polling place ahead of Election Day.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fight to control NJ child care costs continues
item.description: HACKENSACK, N.J. (PIX11) - Elizabeth Velazquez is a mother to three, two of whom go to Little Learners Child Development Center in Hackensack. "My children mean the world to me," she said, holding back tears, "and I want to be able to continue to work hard every day to provide the world to them.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Florida has nearly all ballots counted on Election Day, while California can take weeks. This is why
item.description: Why is it that some states have nearly all the ballots counted within hours of polls closing while others take weeks?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As FEMA prepares for Hurricane Milton, it battles rumors surrounding Helene recovery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The rumors surrounding Hurricane Helene are many. There are false claims that people taking federal relief money could see their land seized. Or that $750 is the most they will ever get to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lombardo among GOP governors criticizing Biden-Harris immigration asylum actions
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo was among 25 Republican governors who signed a letter criticizing the Biden-Harris administration over asylum policies that have brought more than a half-million immigrants into the United States.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Wisconsin Senate race toward GOP
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted the Wisconsin Senate race from "lean Democrat" to "toss up," handing Republican Eric Hovde a boost as he tries to unseat Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.).
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Harris pressed on changing positions, economic plan in '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: Vice President Harris was pressed on her changing policy positions and her expensive economic plan on “60 Minutes,” in what proved to be her toughest media interview since becoming the Democratic nominee.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Poll: In the midst of a tight presidential race, Trump draws support from voters disillusioned with democracy
item.description: New polling shows this year’s Harris v. Trump presidential race is too close to call, with Trump drawing support from voters who are disillusioned with democracy. Results from the McCourtney Institute for Democracy’s Sept. 23-27, 2024 Mood of the Nation Poll.
item.dc:creator: Eric Plutzer and Craig Helmstetter, MPR News

item.title: Harris is pressed for more specifics in a wide-ranging interview with '60 Minutes'
item.description: The interview followed criticism from former President Donald Trump that Kamala Harris has been unwilling to sit for hard interviews. She was pressed on everything from her economic proposals to immigration.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Trump mass deportation plan embraced by some border sheriffs
item.description: Speaking exclusively to NewsNation last week, Trump detailed why he would utilize the expertise of local authorities for his deportation plans.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: Israel has to 'get smart' about supporting him
item.description: Former President Trump said Monday Israel has to “get smart” about supporting him and suggested Jewish people have not adequately backed him after he took several pro-Israel actions while in office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court takes the bench with ghost guns, a capital case and transgender rights on the docket
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court took the bench again on Monday, ready to hear cases on ghost guns, a death sentence and transgender rights.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: GOP senator calls Trump special counsel filing 'a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic'
item.description: Arkansas Sen. Tom Cotton (R) said in a Sunday interview that a recently unsealed filing from special counsel Jack Smith is “a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: New analysis suggests national debt could increase under Harris, but it would surge under Trump
item.description: No one is likely to be happy with the projected higher deficits laid out in a new analysis of Kamala Harris' and Donald Trump's economic plans.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: This year’s Electoral College map may show another ‘blue shift.’ Here’s why
item.description: Differences in how quickly each state’s counties can report this year’s election results may lead to another “blue shift” or “red mirage” on the presidential electoral map.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Walz says he'll 'own up' when he misspeaks as the Democratic ticket steps up media interviews
item.description: The “Fox News Sunday” interview reflected a broader media blitz by presidential nominee Kamala Harris and Walz as the Democrats seek to garner public attention in the final 30 days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in European cities and around the globe, calling for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel approached.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hurricane Milton projected to reach Category 4 strength before landfall in Florida
item.description: A new storm is approaching the west coast of the Florida, threatening to inflict heavy rain, strong winds and flash flooding in the already-storm battered state.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Florida voters concerned about election disruptions after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Polling sites along the coast have lost locations due to Hurricane Helene's damage, and Florida voters are starting to worry about how this could change things for the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris, Trump campaigns send surrogates to Las Vegas to court middle class, Latinos
item.description: With a month until Election Day, Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump sent their surrogates to Las Vegas this week to campaign for Nevada’s six electoral votes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gloria, Turner compete in San Diego mayoral race debate
item.description: The debate between San Diego Mayor Todd Gloria and police officer and challenger Larry Turner covered a wide range of issues from transportation, flood infrastructure failures, housing shortages and of course homelessness.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: ACLU of Nevada attempts to stop purge of nearly 20,000 registered voters
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- The ACLU of Nevada intervened in a court action Wednesday, which attempts to stop the purging of nearly 20,000 registered voters weeks before the presidential election. On Sept.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
